meta.title: Quiz 1 — Web Fundamentals & JavaScript Basics
meta.description: Web history, HTML, HTTP, networking, CSS basics, and JavaScript fundamentals.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)?

options.label: A
options.text: It is a government agency that registers and manages all domain names globally

options.label: B
options.text: It is a company that develops and maintains the Chrome and Firefox web browsers

options.label: C
options.text: It is a university consortium that funds research into network security protocols

options.label: D
options.text: It is the main international standards organization for the World Wide Web

questions.explanationMd: W3C develops web standards and specifications so that the Web remains interoperable across platforms and browsers.

questions.questionMd: In the context of the WWW, what is hypertext?

options.label: A
options.text: A protocol for securely transferring files between computers

options.label: B
options.text: A database format used to store web server logs

options.label: C
options.text: A programming language used to style web page layouts

options.label: D
options.text: A text document that contains links (hyper-links) to other text documents

questions.explanationMd: Hypertext is text containing hyperlinks that let users navigate to related documents or sections.

questions.questionMd: What is the role of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) in relation to HTML?

options.label: A
options.text: SGML is a stylesheet language used to define the visual presentation of XML documents

options.label: B
options.text: SGML is a scripting language that adds interactivity to static HTML pages

options.label: C
options.text: SGML is a standard for specifying markup languages and was used by Tim Berners-Lee to create the HTML specification

options.label: D
options.text: SGML is a transport protocol that HTTP was built upon

questions.explanationMd: HTML was originally defined as an SGML application; SGML itself is a meta-language for defining markup languages.

questions.questionMd: Which keyword is used to create a new instance of an ES6 class?

options.label: A
options.text: init

options.label: B
options.text: create

options.label: C
options.text: instantiate

options.label: D
options.text: new

questions.explanationMd: JavaScript uses the `new` keyword to create an instance from a class or constructor function.

questions.questionMd: How do LANs and WANs relate to the concepts of Intranet and Internet respectively?

options.label: A
options.text: LANs form private internal networks (Intranet), while WANs connect these networks together to form the Internet

options.label: B
options.text: LANs use wireless connections only, while WANs use wired connections only

options.label: C
options.text: LANs are always public networks, while WANs are always private networks

options.label: D
options.text: LANs operate at the application layer, while WANs operate at the physical layer

questions.explanationMd: A LAN typically represents a local private network, while WAN connections link many networks over large geographic areas, enabling the broader Internet.

questions.questionMd: Compared to HTTP/1.1, what is a key advantage of HTTP/2.0?

options.label: A
options.text: HTTP/2.0 encrypts all data by default using SSL certificates

options.label: B
options.text: HTTP/2.0 eliminates the need for DNS lookups before connecting to a server

options.label: C
options.text: HTTP/2.0 keeps the connection open and allows multiple resources to be fetched simultaneously

options.label: D
options.text: HTTP/2.0 only supports text based content and not binary data

questions.explanationMd: HTTP/2 supports multiplexing, allowing multiple streams over a single connection, which improves loading efficiency.

questions.questionMd: The Internet was originally developed in 1969 by which organization to connect computers at research facilities and universities?

options.label: A
options.text: European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN)

options.label: B
options.text: World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)

options.label: C
options.text: Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)

options.label: D
options.text: Transmission Control Protocol Institute (TCPI)

questions.explanationMd: ARPANET, funded by DARPA, is commonly cited as the origin of the modern Internet.

questions.questionMd: Who invented the World Wide Web in 1991 as a way to link research papers using hyperlinks?

options.label: A
options.text: Steve Jobs

options.label: B
options.text: Bill Gates

options.label: C
options.text: Tim Berners-Lee

options.label: D
options.text: Vint Cerf

questions.explanationMd: Tim Berners-Lee created the World Wide Web while working at CERN.

questions.questionMd: Which set of programming languages is associated with front-end web development?

options.label: A
options.text: Python, Java, and Ruby

options.label: B
options.text: SQL, XML, and SGML

options.label: C
options.text: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

options.label: D
options.text: Node.js, PHP, and Go

questions.explanationMd: The traditional front-end stack is HTML for structure, CSS for styling, and JavaScript for behavior.

questions.questionMd: On which TCP port does an HTTP server listen by default?
questions.explanationMd: Port 80 is the standard default port for HTTP traffic.

questions.questionMd: In JavaScript, what is a closure?

options.label: A
options.text: A special syntax for defining private class fields in ES6

options.label: B
options.text: A function declared inside another function that retains access to the outer function's variables

options.label: C
options.text: A technique for combining multiple event listeners into a single handler

options.label: D
options.text: A method that prevents other code from modifying an object's properties

questions.explanationMd: A closure occurs when a function remembers variables from its lexical scope even after the outer function has returned.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `http://www.example.com:8080`, what does the value `8080` represent?

options.label: A
options.text: The TCP port on which the HTTP server is listening

options.label: B
options.text: The subnet mask of the server's IP address

options.label: C
options.text: The version number of the HTTP protocol being used

options.label: D
options.text: The number of packets sent in the HTTP request

questions.explanationMd: The number after the colon in a URL indicates the server port.

questions.questionMd: How are public fields defined on a class instance in ES6?

options.label: A
options.text: By assigning `this.fieldName = value` inside the constructor or a method

options.label: B
options.text: By using the `var` keyword at the top of the class body

options.label: C
options.text: By declaring them with the `public` keyword before the constructor

options.label: D
options.text: By listing them as parameters in the class declaration line

questions.explanationMd: A common ES6 class pattern is to assign instance properties through `this` in the constructor or methods.

questions.questionMd: What is the concept of currying in the context of functional JavaScript?

options.label: A
options.text: Binding an event listener to multiple HTML elements at the same time

options.label: B
options.text: Converting a synchronous function into an asynchronous Promise-based function

options.label: C
options.text: Defining a class method that can accept a variable number of parameters

options.label: D
options.text: Constructing a new function that is partially pre-filled with arguments from an outer function

questions.explanationMd: Currying transforms a function into a sequence of functions that capture arguments step by step.

questions.questionMd: What happens to the variables declared in an outer function when a closure references them, even after the outer function has returned?

options.label: A
options.text: They are kept alive because the closure holds a reference to them

options.label: B
options.text: They are immediately garbage-collected since the outer function has finished

options.label: C
options.text: They are reset to their default values on the next function call

options.label: D
options.text: They become global variables accessible from anywhere in the program

questions.explanationMd: Referenced outer variables stay alive as long as a closure can still access them.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary role of the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP)?

options.label: A
options.text: To assign unique IP addresses to devices on the Internet

options.label: B
options.text: To convert domain names into numerical IP addresses

options.label: C
options.text: To encrypt data transmitted between two computers

options.label: D
options.text: To transport hypertext documents from web servers to client computers

questions.explanationMd: HTTP is the protocol used to transfer web resources such as HTML from servers to clients.

questions.questionMd: When defining methods inside an ES6 class body, which keyword must you omit?

options.label: A
options.text: class

options.label: B
options.text: const

options.label: C
options.text: return

options.label: D
options.text: function

questions.explanationMd: Class methods are declared without the `function` keyword inside a class body.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is an important limitation of the CustomEvent API in JavaScript?

options.label: A
options.text: CustomEvents can only be created inside ES6 class constructors

options.label: B
options.text: CustomEvents can only be dispatched and listened to on HTML elements, not on arbitrary class instances

options.label: C
options.text: CustomEvents cannot carry any additional data in their event object

options.label: D
options.text: CustomEvents are not supported in any browser that uses the V8 engine

questions.explanationMd: The lecture frames CustomEvent as DOM-event oriented rather than a general arbitrary-instance event system.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a characteristic of first-class functions in JavaScript?

options.label: A
options.text: Functions cannot be stored in variables or object properties

options.label: B
options.text: Functions can be passed as parameters to other functions

options.label: C
options.text: Functions must always be declared before the class definition

options.label: D
options.text: Functions require the `function` keyword and cannot use arrow syntax

questions.explanationMd: Because functions are first-class values, they can be stored, passed around, and returned.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) in Internet communication?

options.label: A
options.text: To ensure the integrity of network communication

options.label: B
options.text: To define the visual layout of web pages

options.label: C
options.text: To translate domain names into IP addresses

options.label: D
options.text: To assign unique MAC addresses to network devices

questions.explanationMd: TCP is responsible for reliable, ordered delivery of data between hosts.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is the JavaScript coding convention for indicating that a method is intended to be private?

options.label: A
options.text: Wrapping the method body inside a closure immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Declaring the method with `const` instead of inside the class body

options.label: C
options.text: Prefixing the method name with an underscore, e.g. `_methodName()`

options.label: D
options.text: Using the `private` keyword before the method definition

questions.explanationMd: A leading underscore is a common convention to signal intended privacy, even though it is not enforced by classic syntax.

questions.questionMd: When using the concise arrow function syntax, under what conditions can you omit the curly braces and the `return` keyword?

options.label: A
options.text: When the function takes more than two parameters

options.label: B
options.text: When the function body contains only a single return statement

options.label: C
options.text: When the function is assigned to a variable declared with `var`

options.label: D
options.text: When the function is defined inside a class constructor

questions.explanationMd: Arrow functions allow an implicit return only for a single expression body.

questions.questionMd: What problem does calling `this.methodName = this.methodName.bind(this)` in a constructor solve?

options.label: A
options.text: It copies the method's code into the HTML element for faster execution

options.label: B
options.text: It ensures `this` always refers to the class instance when the method is used as an event handler

options.label: C
options.text: It registers the method as a global function accessible from any scope

options.label: D
options.text: It prevents the method from being called more than once per event

questions.explanationMd: Binding fixes the `this` value so callbacks still refer to the class instance.

questions.questionMd: ES6 classes were introduced in which version of the ECMAScript standard?

options.label: A
options.text: ECMAScript 3 (ES1999)

options.label: B
options.text: ECMAScript 5 (ES2009)

options.label: C
options.text: ECMAScript 8 (ES2017)

options.label: D
options.text: ECMAScript 6 (ES2015)

questions.explanationMd: Class syntax was introduced with ES2015, commonly called ES6.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `http://www.example.com:8088`, what does the value `8088` represent?

options.label: A
options.text: The version number of the HTTP protocol being used

options.label: B
options.text: The number of packets sent in the HTTP request

options.label: C
options.text: The TCP port on which the HTTP server is listening

options.label: D
options.text: The subnet mask of the server's IP address

questions.explanationMd: No explanation provided.

questions.questionMd: Which tag must be used as the root element of an HTML document?

options.label: A
options.text: <head>

options.label: B
options.text: <body>

options.label: C
options.text: <!DOCTYPE html>

options.label: D
options.text: <html>

questions.explanationMd: The `<html>` element is the root element of an HTML page, containing all other elements.

questions.questionMd: How do LANs and WANs relate to the concepts of Intranet and Internet, respectively?

options.label: A
options.text: LANs form private internal networks (Intranets), while WANs connect these networks together to form the Internet

options.label: B
options.text: LANs are strictly used for the Internet, while WANs are strictly used for Intranets

options.label: C
options.text: LANs operate over wireless networks, while WANs operate over wired networks

options.label: D
options.text: Both LANs and WANs are entirely isolated from the global internet

questions.explanationMd: Local Area Networks (LANs) typically make up private corporate Intranets, while Wide Area Networks (WANs) connect these smaller networks globally to form the Internet.

questions.questionMd: Which statement is TRUE regarding the history of HTTP/2?

options.label: A
options.text: HTTP/2 requires encrypted connections by default.

options.label: B
options.text: HTTP/2 removed all headers used in HTTP/1.1.

options.label: C
options.text: HTTP/2 was designed to overcome performance limitations of HTTP/1.1 without changing its semantics.

options.label: D
options.text: HTTP/2 only supports text-based communication, not binary.

questions.explanationMd: HTTP/2 introduced features like multiplexing to speed up page loads without altering the core semantics (methods, status codes, URIs) of HTTP.

questions.questionMd: The Internet was initially created by which organization, under what name, and for what primary purpose?

options.label: A
options.text: European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN)

options.label: B
options.text: World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)

options.label: C
options.text: Department of Defense's Advanced Research Projects Agency (ARPA); ARPANET; military communication and research

options.label: D
options.text: The Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF)

questions.explanationMd: The Internet began as ARPANET, funded by the US Department of Defense's ARPA, to build a robust, fault-tolerant communication network.

questions.questionMd: Who invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at CERN?

options.label: A
options.text: Steve Jobs

options.label: B
options.text: Tim Berners-Lee

options.label: C
options.text: Bill Gates

options.label: D
options.text: Alan Turing

questions.explanationMd: Sir Tim Berners-Lee is credited with inventing the World Wide Web while working at CERN in 1989.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a primary goal of CSS?

options.label: A
options.text: To add interactive logic to web pages

options.label: B
options.text: To query databases for web data

options.label: C
options.text: To separate the presentation of a document from its structure and content

options.label: D
options.text: To define the HTML tags on a page

questions.explanationMd: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) was designed to separate document content (HTML) from document presentation (layout, colors, fonts).

questions.questionMd: Which HTTP method is considered "idempotent" by default?

options.label: A
options.text: POST

options.label: B
options.text: PATCH

options.label: C
options.text: GET

options.label: D
options.text: CONNECT

questions.explanationMd: An idempotent HTTP method is one that can be called many times without producing different outcomes. GET is idempotent; it simply retrieves data.

questions.questionMd: In web development, what is a "port"?

options.label: A
options.text: A physical connection defining hardware interfaces

options.label: B
options.text: A function that holds an IP address

options.label: C
options.text: A communication endpoint mapped to a specific process or network service

options.label: D
options.text: A standard URL parameter

questions.explanationMd: A port is a virtual point where network connections start and end. It is managed by the OS and helps direct traffic to the correct application.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `https://www.example.com:8080`, what does the `8080` represent?

options.label: A
options.text: The TCP port number the server is listening on

options.label: B
options.text: The subnet mask of the server IP address

options.label: C
options.text: The file size allocated for the document request

options.label: D
options.text: The maximum payload size of the HTTP request

questions.explanationMd: The number following the colon after the hostname in a URL specifies the port number on which the server is listening for traffic.

questions.questionMd: How is a static method defined on a class in modern JS?

options.label: A
options.text: By using the `static` keyword before the method name inside the class body

options.label: B
options.text: By using the `@static` decorator before the class definition

options.label: C
options.text: By placing the method outside of the class block

options.label: D
options.text: By listing them as parameters in the class constructor line

questions.explanationMd: In JavaScript, you define static methods by prepending the `static` keyword to the method name within a class definition.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary benefit of the `<canvas>` element in HTML5?

options.label: A
options.text: It acts as a layout container for multiple HTML components at the same time

options.label: B
options.text: It provides a responsive iframe to embed other websites easily

options.label: C
options.text: It handles complex form submissions securely without a server

options.label: D
options.text: It provides a drawing surface that is manipulated via JavaScript for rendering 2D and 3D graphics

questions.explanationMd: The `<canvas>` element acts as a container for graphics, requiring a script (usually JavaScript) to draw the graphics programmatically.

questions.questionMd: What happens to the variables declared inside a function when the function finishes executing (assuming no closures)?

options.label: A
options.text: They are immediately garbage collected after the outer function has finished

options.label: B
options.text: They are kept alive forever just in case the function is called again

options.label: C
options.text: They are saved to LocalStorage by the browser

options.label: D
options.text: They become global variables accessible anywhere in the program

questions.explanationMd: Local variables within a function scope are generally cleared from memory (garbage collected) once the function's execution context ends, unless a closure keeps a reference to them.
